a-drama-in-the-air.label: A Drama in the Air
a-drama-in-the-air.description: A Jules Verne short story in which a balloonist ascending from Frankfort is joined by an uninvited madman who casts out all the ballast to soar toward death.

adelaide.label: Adelaide
adelaide.description: The capital of South Australia, a chief town and port on the castaways' overland crossing of the continent.

aden.label: Aden
aden.description: Red Sea coaling port at the far end of the run from Suez where the Mongolia puts in on Phileas Fogg's way to Bombay.

adriatic-sea.label: Adriatic Sea
adriatic-sea.description: The arm of the Mediterranean between Italy and the Balkan coast, sounded by the Nautilus and dreaded as a drift-goal by Verne's balloonists.

adventures-of-a-special-correspondent.label: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent
adventures-of-a-special-correspondent.description: A Jules Verne novel following a reporter aboard the Grand Transasiatic railway from the Caspian to Peking, among them a stowaway hidden in a packing case.

africa.label: Africa
africa.description: The great continent of unknown interior that Dr. Ferguson sets out to traverse by balloon from east to west in Five Weeks in a Balloon.

albatross-abduction.label: The Abduction by the Albatross
aliases: The Kidnapping of Prudent and Evans

albatross.label: Albatross
albatross.description: Robur's heavier-than-air flying machine, lifted by dozens of screws, in Robur the Conqueror.

aleutian-islands.label: Aleutian Islands
aleutian-islands.description: The real island chain the drifting colonists of The Fur Country hope to reach as their last landfall before the open Pacific.

alexandria.label: Alexandria
alexandria.description: The Egyptian Mediterranean port and mail-route landfall, a stage on the sea roads toward Suez, Aden, and the East in Verne.

algiers.label: Algiers
algiers.description: The chief city of the North African coast, built amphitheater-wise above its bay, swallowed with the coast when a fragment of earth breaks away.

allahabad.label: Allahabad
allahabad.description: Indian railway town where the Great Indian Peninsula line resumes, forcing Fogg's elephant journey across the gap from Kholby.
allahabad.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

alps.label: Alps
alps.description: The great mountain wall of central Europe, giant of the continent crowned by Mont Blanc, a measure against which Verne weighs all other heights.

altamont-harbour.label: Altamont Harbour
altamont-harbour.description: The port on the New America coast named for Altamont, the northern base from which Hatteras's party sails toward the Pole.

amou-daria.label: Amou-Daria
amou-daria.description: The great Central Asian river, the Oxus of the Ancients, crossed by the Grand Trans-Asian railway in Verne and rival of the Indus and Ganges.

amsterdam-island.label: Amsterdam Island
amsterdam-island.description: The real Indian Ocean island, a solitary cone on the 37th parallel, weighed and rejected as a refuge by the searchers in In Search of the Castaways.

an-antarctic-mystery.label: An Antarctic Mystery
aliases: The Sphinx of the Ice Fields
an-antarctic-mystery.description: A Jules Verne novel, sequel to Poe's Arthur Gordon Pym, in which the schooner Halbrane sails into the Antarctic to seek the survivors of a lost ship.

andes-earthquake.label: The Andes Earthquake
andes-earthquake.description: While crossing the Cordillera of the Andes, Lord Glenarvan's search party is caught in an earthquake that hurls them down the mountainside and sweeps young Robert Grant away.

andes.label: Andes
andes.description: The great mountain chain of South America, the Cordilleras that Glenarvan's party crossed on the 37th parallel in search of Captain Grant.

angola.label: Angola
angola.description: Portuguese colony on Africa's western coast, crossed by slave caravans, where Dick Sand's party is wrecked in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen.
angola.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

antarctic-sea.label: Antarctic Sea
antarctic-sea.description: The ice-strewn southern ocean into which the schooner Halbrane sails on her polar quest in An Antarctic Mystery.

aouda.label: Aouda
aouda.description: The young Parsee widow rescued by Phileas Fogg during his journey across India.
aouda.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

arabia.label: Arabia
arabia.description: The great peninsula the Nautilus skirts in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea, its Red Sea coasts and ancient canal-lands threaded between Suez and the Persian Gulf.
arabia.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

aral-sea.label: Aral Sea
aral-sea.description: The shrinking inland sea of Central Asia, drying like the Caspian it was once joined to, crossed in Verne's Trans-Asian rail and airship journeys.
aral-sea.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

arctic-ocean.label: Arctic Ocean
arctic-ocean.description: The northern polar ocean whose ice-bound shores frame the expedition of The Fur Country and the voyages of Captain Hatteras.

argentine.label: Argentine
argentine.description: The South American republic whose plains and Pampas the searchers cross on the thirty-seventh parallel in In Search of the Castaways, from the Cordilleras down to the Atlantic.

aronnax.label: Professor Aronnax

around-the-world-in-eighty-days.label: Around the World in Eighty Days
around-the-world-in-eighty-days.description: A Jules Verne novel in which Phileas Fogg wagers his fortune that he can circle the globe in eighty days.

AWED-ch23.description: IN WHICH PASSEPARTOUT'S NOSE BECOMES OUTRAGEOUSLY LONG
AWED-ch23.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch24.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXIV
AWED-ch24.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch25.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXV
AWED-ch25.description: IN WHICH A SLIGHT GLIMPSE IS HAD OF SAN FRANCISCO
AWED-ch25.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch26.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXVI
AWED-ch26.description: IN WHICH PHILEAS FOGG AND PARTY TRAVEL BY THE PACIFIC RAILROAD
AWED-ch26.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch27.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXVII
AWED-ch27.description: IN WHICH PASSEPARTOUT UNDERGOES, AT A SPEED OF TWENTY MILES AN HOUR, A COURSE OF MORMON HISTORY
AWED-ch27.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch28.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXVIII
AWED-ch28.description: IN WHICH PASSEPARTOUT DOES NOT SUCCEED IN MAKING ANYBODY LISTEN TO REASON
AWED-ch28.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch29.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXIX
AWED-ch29.description: IN WHICH CERTAIN INCIDENTS ARE NARRATED WHICH ARE ONLY TO BE MET WITH ON AMERICAN RAILROADS
AWED-ch29.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch30.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXX
AWED-ch30.description: IN WHICH PHILEAS FOGG SIMPLY DOES HIS DUTY
AWED-ch30.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch31.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXXI
AWED-ch31.description: IN WHICH FIX, THE DETECTIVE, CONSIDERABLY FURTHERS THE INTERESTS OF PHILEAS FOGG
AWED-ch31.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch32.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXXII
AWED-ch32.description: IN WHICH PHILEAS FOGG ENGAGES IN A DIRECT STRUGGLE WITH BAD FORTUNE
AWED-ch32.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch33.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXXIII
AWED-ch33.description: IN WHICH PHILEAS FOGG SHOWS HIMSELF EQUAL TO THE OCCASION
AWED-ch33.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch34.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXXIV
AWED-ch34.description: IN WHICH PHILEAS FOGG AT LAST REACHES LONDON
AWED-ch34.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch35.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXXV
AWED-ch35.description: IN WHICH PHILEAS FOGG DOES NOT HAVE TO REPEAT HIS ORDERS TO PASSEPARTOUT TWICE
AWED-ch35.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch36.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXXVI
AWED-ch36.description: IN WHICH PHILEAS FOGG‘S NAME IS ONCE MORE AT A PREMIUM ON ’CHANGE
AWED-ch36.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

AWED-ch37.label: Around the World in Eighty Days — CHAPTER XXXVII
AWED-ch37.description: IN WHICH IT IS SHOWN THAT PHILEAS FOGG GAINED NOTHING BY HIS TOUR AROUND THE WORLD, UNLESS IT WERE HAPPINESS
AWED-ch37.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

a-winter-amid-the-ice.label: A Winter Amid the Ice
aliases: A Winter's Sojourn in the Ice
a-winter-amid-the-ice.description: A Jules Verne novella in which an old Dunkirk sailor voyages into the Arctic to rescue his shipwrecked son, wintering amid the ice against treachery and polar bears.

axel.label: Axel
axel.description: The young nephew who narrates the descent to the centre of the earth.
axel.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

ayrton.label: Ayrton
ayrton.description: A repentant traitor, once quartermaster of the Britannia, redeemed as the sixth colonist of Lincoln Island.

back-cup.label: Back Cup
back-cup.description: A hollow volcanic islet in the Bermudas that serves as the secret lair of the pirate Ker Karraje in Facing the Flag.

baffin-bay.label: Baffin Bay
baffin-bay.description: The ice-filled Arctic sea between Greenland and Baffin Island, the first great barrier crossed by the Forward in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras.

baffins-bay.label: Baffins Bay
baffins-bay.description: The great Arctic sea on the west of Greenland, a haunt of whalers and the mouth of the long suite of straits leading toward the North-West Passage.

baku.label: Baku
baku.description: The Caspian oil-port of the Guebres, eastern terminus of the Transcaucasian railway where the reporter embarks across Asia.
baku.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

baltimore.label: Baltimore
baltimore.description: The Maryland city where the Gun Club is established in From the Earth to the Moon.
baltimore.book: From the Earth to the Moon

baraba.label: Baraba
baraba.description: A vast swampy steppe district of Western Siberia between the Obi and the Irtych, dreaded for its marshes and insects in Michael Strogoff.

barbicane.label: Impey Barbicane
barbicane.description: The resolute president of the Gun Club who launches the projectile to the Moon.
barbicane.book: From the Earth to the Moon

barrow-strait.label: Barrow Strait
barrow-strait.description: The real Arctic strait beyond Lancaster Strait, the well-trodden approach to Beechey Island that the Forward follows and later flees back through.

bay-of-bengal.label: Bay of Bengal
bay-of-bengal.description: The great bay of the Indian Ocean crossed by the Nautilus, named among the world's pearl fisheries.
bay-of-bengal.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

bay-of-talcahuano.label: Bay of Talcahuano
bay-of-talcahuano.description: The Chilean estuary where the yacht Duncan begins and ends her search for Captain Grant along the thirty-seventh parallel in In Search of the Castaways.

beechey-island.label: Beechey Island
beechey-island.description: The real Arctic island and rendezvous of polar expeditions in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras, where Hatteras counts on renewing the Forward's coal.

behring-sea.label: Behring Sea
behring-sea.description: The real northern sea into which the drifting Victoria Island is carried in The Fur Country, scattered with the islands that offer the colonists their last hope of rescue.

behring-strait.label: Behring Strait
behring-strait.description: The real strait between Asia and America toward which the drifting ice-island is carried in The Fur Country, the colony's dreaded destination and hoped-for lifeline.

belem.label: Belem
belem.description: The Brazilian city at the mouth of the Amazon, terminus of the jangada voyage in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
belem.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

belgium.label: Belgium
belgium.description: The small but generous Western European kingdom, a land of grottoes and railways threaded through Verne's Continental journeys.
belgium.book: From the Earth to the Moon

bellot-strait.label: Bellot Strait
bellot-strait.description: The real narrow Arctic strait on which Hatteras stakes his voyage, the only southern outlet from the Gulf of Boothia.

benares.label: Benares
benares.description: Sacred city on the Ganges and Sir Francis Cromarty's destination on Phileas Fogg's railway crossing of India.
benares.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

bennet-islet.label: Bennet Islet
bennet-islet.description: A small, barren island of the far southern seas in An Antarctic Mystery, a landfall on the Halbrane's search for the men of the Jane.

bergen.label: Bergen
bergen.description: The Norwegian west-coast port and fishing home of Ole Kamp, whose ship the Viking sails from it in Ticket No. 9672.

berlin.label: Berlin
berlin.description: The German capital, a seat of learning and diplomacy repeatedly invoked in Verne's global affairs.

bihe.label: Bihe
bihe.description: A slave-trade market town of the Benguela interior, one of the trader Alvez's factories in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen.
bihe.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

black-rock-creek.label: Black Rock Creek
black-rock-creek.description: The wild creek on the shore of Lake Erie that serves as a hiding place for Robur's machine the Terror in The Master of the World.

black-sea.label: Black Sea
black-sea.description: Great inland sea between Europe and Asia Minor, recurring across Verne's works as a landing point and a fixed name of geography and trade.
black-sea.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

blue-ridge-mountains.label: Blue Ridge Mountains
blue-ridge-mountains.description: The Appalachian range whose peak the Great Eyrie touches off the mystery of The Master of the World.

bokhara.label: Bokhara
bokhara.description: The ancient Central Asian city and khanate, the "Rome of Islam," a great oasis station on the Grand Transasiatic railway.
bokhara.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

bolivia.label: Bolivia
bolivia.description: The South American land that the traitor Harris pretends the shipwrecked survivors have reached in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen, when in truth they stand on Angola.
bolivia.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

bombay.label: Bombay
bombay.description: Indian port where Phileas Fogg lands and boards the railway across the subcontinent.
bombay.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

bordeaux.label: Bordeaux
bordeaux.description: The French Atlantic port on the Gironde, a departure point and landfall for Verne's globe-trotters and aeronauts.
bordeaux.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

boston.label: Boston
boston.description: The New England seaport off which a mysterious craft is sighted in Master of the World, and home city of several of Verne's American characters.

BR-ch03.label: The Blockade Runners — CHAPTER III
BR-ch03.description: THINGS ARE NOT WHAT THEY SEEM

BR-ch05.label: The Blockade Runners — CHAPTER V
BR-ch05.description: THE SHOT FROM THE IROQUOIS, AND MISS JENNY'S ARGUMENTS

brazil.label: Brazil
brazil.description: The great South American empire whose provinces the Amazon waters, home of the Garral family in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
brazil.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

brest.label: Brest
brest.description: The great French naval port on the Atlantic, the Alaska's landfall in her rescue voyage and a cradle of Verne's sea expeditions.

buffalo.label: Buffalo
buffalo.description: The New York port at the eastern end of Lake Erie, past which the Terror races toward Niagara in The Master of the World.

calais.label: Calais
calais.description: The French Channel port facing Dover, a crossing point for Verne's globe-trotters and pioneering aeronauts.
calais.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

calcutta.label: Calcutta
calcutta.description: Bengal capital and eastern terminus of Phileas Fogg's railway crossing of India.
calcutta.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

california.label: California
california.description: The Pacific-coast American state, capital San Francisco, from which Godfrey Morgan sets out to sea in Godfrey Morgan.

callao.label: Callao
callao.description: The Pacific seaport of Lima in Peru, a setting in The Pearl of Lima and the last recorded port of the Britannia in In Search of the Castaways.

cambridge-observatory.label: Cambridge Observatory
cambridge-observatory.description: The Massachusetts observatory whose director answers the Gun Club's astronomical questions about reaching the Moon.
cambridge-observatory.book: From the Earth to the Moon

canada.label: Canada
canada.description: The northern fur-trade dominion, once French then British, that forms the base of the Hudson Bay expedition in The Fur Country.

canadian-general-transportation-company.label: Canadian General Transportation Company
canadian-general-transportation-company.description: The defunct Canadian shipping firm that owned the lost steamer Cynthia, at the heart of Erik's search for his origins.

cape-bathurst.label: Cape Bathurst
cape-bathurst.description: An Arctic promontory north of the seventieth parallel where Lieutenant Hobson founds Fort Hope in The Fur Country.

cape-bernouilli.label: Cape Bernouilli
cape-bernouilli.description: The cape on the South Australian coast where the 37th parallel meets the continent, the starting point of the Duncan party's overland crossing in In Search of the Castaways.

cape-bon.label: Cape Bon
cape-bon.description: Northernmost promontory of Africa and nearest point to Sicily, prominent in the reshaped Mediterranean of Off on a Comet.

cape-corrientes.label: Cape Corrientes
cape-corrientes.description: The cape on the Argentine coast where the Duncan cruises and rides out a storm in In Search of the Castaways.

cape-esquimaux.label: Cape Esquimaux
cape-esquimaux.description: The fictional headland on the drifting peninsula of Cape Bathurst in The Fur Country, worn away by the sea as the ice-island melts.

cape-farewell.label: Cape Farewell
cape-farewell.description: The real southern cape of Greenland, the Forward's first great landmark on its secret course toward the Pole.

cape-horn.label: Cape Horn
cape-horn.description: The rock at the southern tip of South America, doubled by ships rounding between the Atlantic and Pacific in several of the Voyages.
cape-horn.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

cape-michael.label: Cape Michael
cape-michael.description: The fictional headland on the drifting peninsula of Cape Bathurst in The Fur Country, eastern limit of the colony and the point where the settlers quit the doomed island.

cape-of-good-hope.label: Cape of Good Hope
cape-of-good-hope.description: The southern cape of Africa, one of the great capes the Nautilus must double on its course between the Indian Ocean and the Atlantic in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
cape-of-good-hope.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

cape-saknussemm.label: Cape Saknussemm
cape-saknussemm.description: The cape on the shore of the subterranean Lidenbrock Sea, named for Arne Saknussemm, from which the explorers are swept north in A Journey to the Center of the Earth.
cape-saknussemm.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

cape-tchelynskin.label: Cape Tchelynskin
cape-tchelynskin.description: The northernmost cape of the Eurasian mainland, the great obstacle the Alaska must double on her polar voyage in The Waif of the Cynthia.

cape-verde.label: Cape Verde
cape-verde.description: The volcanic archipelago off West Africa, a waypoint on the Duncan's voyage and, for Captain Nemo, a surviving peak of drowned Atlantis.

cape-washington.label: Cape Washington
cape-washington.description: A headland on the coast of New America named by Altamont, marking the route between the sea and Fort Providence in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras.

captain-hatteras.label: Captain Hatteras
captain-hatteras.description: The obsessive English captain driven to plant his flag at the North Pole.

captain-nemo.label: Captain Nemo
captain-nemo.description: The enigmatic commander of the Nautilus who has renounced human society.
captain-nemo.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

caribbean-sea.label: Caribbean Sea
caribbean-sea.description: The clear tropical sea whose transparent depths and teeming life the Nautilus explores in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
caribbean-sea.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

carmen.label: Carmen
carmen.description: A colonial town of Patagonia at the mouth of the Rio Negro, terminus of the Carmen-to-Mendoza route in In Search of the Castaways.

caspian-sea.label: Caspian Sea
caspian-sea.description: The great inland sea of Central Asia whose eastern port of Uzun Ada begins the trans-Asian railway journey in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
caspian-sea.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

cassange.label: Cassange
cassange.description: A slave-trade market town of the Benguela interior, one of the trader Alvez's factories in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen.
cassange.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

caucasus.label: Caucasus
caucasus.description: The mountain barrier between European Russia and Transcaucasia, crossed by Verne's couriers and correspondents.

ceuta.label: Ceuta
ceuta.description: Fortified point on the Moroccan coast opposite Gibraltar, carried off by the comet Gallia in Off on a Comet.

ceylon.label: Ceylon
ceylon.description: The pearl-fishery island off the Indian peninsula where the Nautilus takes Aronnax to the oyster banks in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
ceylon.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

chancellor-fire.label: The Burning of the Chancellor
chancellor-fire.description: A cargo of cotton smouldering deep in the hold of the Chancellor breaks into an unquenchable fire, dooming the ship and driving her people toward their long ordeal at sea.

charleston-blockade-run.label: The Running of the Charleston Blockade
aliases: The Forcing of the Charleston Blockade

charleston.label: Charleston
charleston.description: The South Carolina port besieged and blockaded in the Civil War, the destination of the daring blockade runners.

chatham-islands.label: Chatham Islands
chatham-islands.description: The remote South Pacific island group where the Albatross anchors and its captive passengers attempt escape in Robur the Conqueror.

chicago.label: Chicago
chicago.description: The great American railway city on Lake Michigan, a key waypoint on Phileas Fogg's homeward journey in Around the World in Eighty Days.
chicago.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

chili.label: Chili
chili.description: The western South American republic (Verne's spelling of Chile) where Glenarvan's overland search across the Cordilleras and Pampas begins in In Search of the Castaways.

china.label: China
china.description: The eastern terminus of the Grand Transasiatic railway, destination of the reporter Bombarnac in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
china.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

christiania-lottery-drawing.label: The Christiania Lottery Drawing
aliases: The Drawing of the Great Lottery

christiania.label: Christiania
christiania.description: The Norwegian capital (today Oslo) whose professor Sylvius Hogg befriends the Hansen family in Ticket No. 9672.

christmas-harbour.label: Christmas Harbour
christmas-harbour.description: The real Kerguelen harbour where An Antarctic Mystery opens, home of the Green Cormorant inn and the mooring of the schooner Halbrane.

claw-cape.label: Claw Cape
claw-cape.description: The fictional yataghan-shaped headland at the southeastern extremity of Lincoln Island in The Mysterious Island, named by Neb during the settlers' survey.

coal-town.label: Coal Town
coal-town.description: The subterranean mining village lit by electricity beneath Loch Katrine, home of the New Aberfoyle miners in The Underground City.

coanza.label: Coanza
coanza.description: One of the rivers of Angola, artery of the Benguela slave country in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen.
coanza.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

colombia.label: Colombia
colombia.description: The northern South American republic that disputes the Amazon's paternity in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon, its coasts and caverns recurring across Verne's works.
colombia.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

columbiad-launch.label: The Columbiad Launch
columbiad-launch.description: The firing of the Columbiad cannon that hurls the projectile toward the Moon.
columbiad-launch.book: From the Earth to the Moon

columbiad.label: Columbiad
columbiad.description: The 900-foot cast-iron cannon built by the Baltimore Gun Club to fire a manned projectile to the Moon in Jules Verne's From the Earth to the Moon.
columbiad.book: From the Earth to the Moon

comet-collision.label: The Comet's Collision
comet-collision.description: A wandering comet grazes the Earth on New Year's night, tearing away a fragment of the globe that carries a band of survivors into space as the new world Gallia.

concepcion.label: Concepcion
concepcion.description: An old city of Chile near the Pacific coast, the landfall from which the 37th-parallel search begins in In Search of the Castaways.

condor-abduction.label: The Condor Abduction
condor-abduction.description: After the Andes earthquake a giant condor seizes the seemingly lifeless Robert Grant and bears him aloft, until a marksman's shot brings the bird down and the boy is recovered alive.

congo.label: Congo
congo.description: The great West African river at whose mouth the castaways of Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen come ashore, later proven to be the lower course of the Loualaba.
congo.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

connecticut.label: Connecticut
connecticut.description: New England state, home of Jeorling, the narrator of An Antarctic Mystery.

conseil.label: Conseil
conseil.description: The devoted, classification-obsessed manservant of Professor Aronnax aboard the Nautilus.
conseil.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

constantinople.label: Constantinople
constantinople.description: The historic Ottoman capital on the Bosphorus, a byword in Verne for siege artillery and daring aeronauts.
constantinople.book: From the Earth to the Moon

copenhagen.label: Copenhagen
copenhagen.description: The Danish capital and seat of northern learning from which the professor sets out for Iceland and the descent into the earth.
copenhagen.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

coppermine-river.label: Coppermine River
coppermine-river.description: A large river of Arctic North America flowing north into Coronation Gulf, a landmark of the expedition in The Fur Country.

coronation-gulf.label: Coronation Gulf
coronation-gulf.description: The Arctic estuary of the North American coast that the fur-hunting expedition skirts in The Fur Country.

corral.label: The Corral
corral.description: The livestock enclosure near the Red Creek on Lincoln Island, later Ayrton's dwelling.

corsica.label: Corsica
corsica.description: The mountainous Mediterranean island of granite and sea-caves, a familiar measure of size and place in Verne's seas.

creek-glycerine.label: Creek Glycerine
creek-glycerine.description: The small artificial stream cut by the colonists of Lincoln Island to drain Lake Grant, guarding the approach to Prospect Heights.

crespo-island.label: Crespo Island
crespo-island.description: A Pacific islet whose submerged forests serve as Captain Nemo's undersea hunting ground in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas.
crespo-island.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

crete.label: Crete
crete.description: The great Mediterranean island, also called Candia, in rebellion against the Ottoman Empire as the Nautilus passed its waters.
crete.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

cyrus-harding.label: Cyrus Harding
cyrus-harding.description: The resourceful engineer who leads the castaways of Lincoln Island.

DA.label: A Drama in the Air
DA.description: In the month of September, 185--, I arrived at Frankfort-on-the-Maine.

date-line-revelation.label: The Gained Day
date-line-revelation.description: Fogg discovers he has unknowingly gained a day by travelling eastward, and has won the wager.
date-line-revelation.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

davis-strait.label: Davis Strait
davis-strait.description: The strait between Greenland and Baffin Island through which the Forward passes to reach Baffin Bay in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras.

death-of-martin-paz.label: The Death of Martin Paz
aliases: The Rescue at the Madeira Cataract

death-of-master-zacharius.label: The Death of Master Zacharius
aliases: The Bursting of the Last Clock

denmark.label: Denmark
denmark.description: One of the six polar powers of Topsy-Turvy, whose Arctic possessions and Copenhagen approaches recur across Verne's voyages.

detroit-river.label: Detroit River
detroit-river.description: The strait between Lake Erie and Lake Huron by which the Terror flees its pursuers in The Master of the World.

dick-sand-a-captain-at-fifteen.label: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen
dick-sand-a-captain-at-fifteen.description: A Jules Verne novel in which a fifteen-year-old novice must command a schooner after its crew is lost, only to be steered by treachery onto the African coast.

dick-sand.label: Dick Sand
dick-sand.description: A fifteen-year-old apprentice who takes command of the Pilgrim after its captain is lost.
dick-sand.book: Dick Sand, A Captain at Fifteen

disintegration-of-victoria-island.label: The Disintegration of Victoria Island
aliases: The End of the Ice Island

diving-apparatus.label: Rouquayrol Diving Apparatus
diving-apparatus.description: The self-contained diving suit and air tank used by Captain Nemo's crew for untethered underwater walks in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
diving-apparatus.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

DOSE-ch01.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER I
DOSE-ch01.description: HOW IT IS USELESS TO SEEK, EVEN ON THE BEST MAPS, FOR THE SMALL TOWN OF QUIQUENDONE.

DOSE-ch02.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER II
DOSE-ch02.description: IN WHICH THE BURGOMASTER VAN TRICASSE AND THE COUNSELLOR NIKLAUSSE CONSULT ABOUT THE AFFAIRS OF THE TOWN.

DOSE-ch03.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER III
DOSE-ch03.description: IN WHICH THE COMMISSARY PASSAUF ENTERS AS NOISILY AS UNEXPECTEDLY.

DOSE-ch04.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER IV
DOSE-ch04.description: IN WHICH DOCTOR OX REVEALS HIMSELF AS A PHYSIOLOGIST OF THE FIRST RANK, AND AS AN AUDACIOUS EXPERIMENTALIST.

DOSE-ch05.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER V
DOSE-ch05.description: IN WHICH THE BURGOMASTER AND THE COUNSELLOR PAY A VISIT TO DOCTOR OX, AND WHAT FOLLOWS.

DOSE-ch06.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER VI
DOSE-ch06.description: IN WHICH FRANTZ NIKLAUSSE AND SUZEL VAN TRICASSE FORM CERTAIN PROJECTS FOR THE FUTURE.

DOSE-ch07.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER VII
DOSE-ch07.description: IN WHICH THE ANDANTES BECOME ALLEGROS, AND THE ALLEGROS VIVACES.

DOSE-ch08.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER VIII
DOSE-ch08.description: IN WHICH THE ANCIENT AND SOLEMN GERMAN WALTZ BECOMES A WHIRLWIND.

DOSE-ch09.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER IX
DOSE-ch09.description: IN WHICH DOCTOR OX AND YGÈNE, HIS ASSISTANT, SAY A FEW WORDS.

DOSE-ch10.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER X
DOSE-ch10.description: IN WHICH IT WILL BE SEEN THAT THE EPIDEMIC INVADES THE ENTIRE TOWN, AND WHAT EFFECT IT PRODUCES.

DOSE-ch11.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER XI
DOSE-ch11.description: IN WHICH THE QUIQUENDONIANS ADOPT A HEROIC RESOLUTION.

DOSE-ch12.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER XII
DOSE-ch12.description: IN WHICH YGÈNE, THE ASSISTANT, GIVES A REASONABLE PIECE OF ADVICE, WHICH IS EAGERLY REJECTED BY DOCTOR OX.

DOSE-ch13.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER XIII
DOSE-ch13.description: IN WHICH IT IS ONCE MORE PROVED THAT BY TAKING HIGH GROUND ALL HUMAN LITTLENESSES MAY BE OVERLOOKED.

DOSE-ch14.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER XIV
DOSE-ch14.description: IN WHICH MATTERS GO SO FAR THAT THE INHABITANTS OF QUIQUENDONE, THE READER, AND EVEN THE AUTHOR, DEMAND AN IMMEDIATE DÉNOUEMENT.

DOSE-ch15.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER XV
DOSE-ch15.description: IN WHICH THE DÉNOUEMENT TAKES PLACE.

DOSE-ch16.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment — CHAPTER XVI
DOSE-ch16.description: IN WHICH THE INTELLIGENT READER SEES THAT HE HAS GUESSED CORRECTLY, DESPITE ALL THE AUTHOR'S PRECAUTIONS.

DOSE-ch17.label: Doctor Ox‘s Experiment — CHAPTER XVII. IN WHICH DOCTOR OX’S THEORY IS EXPLAINED
DOSE-ch17.description: What, then, had this mysterious Doctor Ox done?

doctor-oxs-experiment.label: Doctor Ox's Experiment
doctor-oxs-experiment.description: A Jules Verne satirical novella in which a scientist secretly floods a placid Flemish town with pure oxygen to see what excitement it will breed.

frankfort.description: The German fair-city on the Main from which the narrator makes his fateful balloon ascent amid the crowds of the September fair.

from-the-earth-to-the-moon.description: A Jules Verne novel in which the Baltimore Gun Club casts a giant cannon to fire a manned projectile at the Moon.

fulgurator.label: Fulgurator
fulgurator.description: The self-propelled super-explosive war-engine invented by Thomas Roch, whose secret drives the plot of Facing the Flag.

FWB-ch18.description: The Karagwah.--Lake Ukereoue.--A Night on an Island.--The Equator.-- Crossing the Lake.--The Cascades.--A View of the Country.--The Sources of the Nil

FWB-ch26.description: One Hundred and Thirteen Degrees.--The Doctor's Reflections.--A Desperate Search.--The Cylinder goes out.--One Hundred and Twenty-two Degrees.-- Conte

FWB-ch27.description: Terrific Heat.--Hallucinations.--The Last Drops of Water.--Nights of Despair.--An Attempt at Suicide.--The Simoom.--The Oasis.--The Lion and Lioness.

FWB-ch35.description: What happened to Joe.--The Island of the Biddiomahs.--The Adoration shown him.--The Island that sank.--The Shores of the Lake.--The Tree of the Serpen

FWB-ch36.description: A Throng of People on the Horizon.--A Troop of Arabs.--The Pursuit.

FWB-ch39.description: The Country in the Elbow of the Niger.--A Fantastic View of the Hombori Mountains.--Kabra.--Timbuctoo.--The Chart of Dr.

gallia.label: Gallia
gallia.description: The fragment of Earth carried off around the sun as a new little planet, the travelling world of Off on a Comet.

gallian-sea.label: Gallian Sea
gallian-sea.description: The sea covering half the surface of the comet Gallia, carrying the fragment of the Mediterranean off into space in Off on a Comet.

ganges.label: Ganges
ganges.description: The sacred river of India, artery of Fogg's crossing by rail and steamboat and a stream of the Hindu dead in Verne's East.
ganges.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

geneva.label: Geneva
geneva.description: The lakeside Swiss city of Master Zacharius, home of the old clockmaker whose watches betray him, set before the town was given over to the dryness of Calvinism.

germany.label: Germany
germany.description: The Central European homeland of Professor Lidenbrock in A Journey to the Center of the Earth, and a recurring byword for learning and skilled craft across Verne's works.
germany.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

giant-squid-attack.label: The Giant Squid Attack
giant-squid-attack.description: The battle of the Nautilus crew against giant cuttlefish beneath the sea.
giant-squid-attack.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

giant-telescope.label: Giant Telescope
giant-telescope.description: The gigantic reflecting telescope erected on Long's Peak to observe the Gun Club's lunar projectile in From the Earth to the Moon.
giant-telescope.book: From the Earth to the Moon

gibraltar.label: Gibraltar
gibraltar.description: Rock at the mouth of the Mediterranean, a surviving fragment carried off by the comet Gallia.

gideon-spilett.label: Gideon Spilett
gideon-spilett.description: War correspondent of the New York Herald and one of the five castaways of Lincoln Island.

glenarvan.label: Lord Glenarvan
glenarvan.description: The Scottish nobleman and owner of the yacht Duncan who leads the expedition to rescue the shipwrecked Captain Grant.

GM-ch01.description: IN WHICH THE READER HAS THE OPPORTUNITY OF BUYING AN ISLAND IN THE PACIFIC OCEAN.

GM-ch03.description: THE CONVERSATION OF PHINA HOLLANEY AND GODFREY MORGAN, WITH A PIANO ACCOMPANIMENT.

GM-ch05.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER V
GM-ch05.description: IN WHICH THEY PREPARE TO GO, AND AT THE END OF WHICH THEY GO FOR GOOD.

GM-ch06.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER VI
GM-ch06.description: IN WHICH THE READER MAKES THE ACQUAINTANCE OF A NEW PERSONAGE.

GM-ch08.description: WHICH LEADS GODFREY TO BITTER REFLECTIONS ON THE MANIA FOR TRAVELLING.

GM-ch09.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER IX
GM-ch09.description: IN WHICH IT IS SHOWN THAT CRUSOES DO NOT HAVE EVERYTHING AS THEY WISH.

GM-ch10.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER X
GM-ch10.description: IN WHICH GODFREY DOES WHAT ANY OTHER SHIPWRECKED MAN WOULD HAVE DONE UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES.

GM-ch11.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER XI
GM-ch11.description: IN WHICH THE QUESTION OF LODGING IS SOLVED AS WELL AS IT COULD BE.

GM-ch13.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER XIII
GM-ch13.description: IN WHICH GODFREY AGAIN SEES A SLIGHT SMOKE OVER ANOTHER PART OF THE ISLAND.

GM-ch14.description: WHEREIN GODFREY FINDS SOME WRECKAGE, TO WHICH HE AND HIS COMPANION GIVE A HEARTY WELCOME.

GM-ch15.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER XV
GM-ch15.description: IN WHICH THERE HAPPENS WHAT HAPPENS AT LEAST ONCE IN THE LIFE OF EVERY CRUSOE, REAL OR IMAGINARY.

GM-ch16.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER XVI
GM-ch16.description: IN WHICH SOMETHING HAPPENS WHICH CANNOT FAIL TO SURPRISE THE READER.

GM-ch17.description: IN WHICH PROFESSOR TARTLET'S GUN REALLY DOES MARVELS.

GM-ch18.description: WHICH TREATS OF THE MORAL AND PHYSICAL EDUCATION OF A SIMPLE NATIVE OF THE PACIFIC.

GM-ch19.description: IN WHICH THE SITUATION ALREADY GRAVELY COMPROMISED BECOMES MORE AND MORE COMPLICATED.

GM-ch20.description: IN WHICH TARTLET REITERATES IN EVERY KEY THAT HE WOULD RATHER BE OFF.

GM-ch21.description: WHICH ENDS WITH QUITE A SURPRISING REFLECTION BY THE NEGRO CAREFINOTU.

GM-ch22.label: Godfrey Morgan — CHAPTER XXII
GM-ch22.description: WHICH CONCLUDES BY EXPLAINING WHAT UP TO NOW HAD APPEARED INEXPLICABLE.

go-ahead.label: Go-Ahead
go-ahead.description: The giant dirigible aerostat built by the Weldon Institute to vindicate lighter-than-air flight against Robur's Albatross.

goat-island.label: Goat Island
goat-island.description: The wooded island in the Niagara River dividing the American falls from the Canadian, appearing in The Master of the World and Robur the Conqueror.

gobi.label: Gobi
gobi.description: Great sandy desert of Central Asia crossed by the Grand Transasiatic railway in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
gobi.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

godfrey-morgan.label: Godfrey Morgan
godfrey-morgan.description: A Jules Verne novel in which a rich young Californian gets his wish for a castaway adventure, never knowing his ordeal has been staged by his uncle.

gondokoro.label: Gondokoro
gondokoro.description: A station on the Nile between the fourth and fifth parallels of north latitude, the northern limit of river exploration in Five Weeks in a Balloon.

gourbi-island.label: Gourbi Island
gourbi-island.description: The fictional island on the comet Gallia in Off on a Comet, the last inhabited shred of Algeria carried into space and base of the castaways.

grand-transasiatic-railway-company.label: Grand Transasiatic Railway Company
grand-transasiatic-railway-company.description: The newly opened railway company running the through line from the Caspian to Peking, along which the whole action of Claudius Bombarnac unfolds.
grand-transasiatic-railway-company.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

granite-house-lift.label: Granite House Lift
granite-house-lift.description: The water-powered lift built by Cyrus Harding to raise the colonists to their cliff-dwelling in The Mysterious Island.

granite-house.label: Granite House
granite-house.description: The cliff-cavern dwelling that the castaways make their home in The Mysterious Island.

great-bear-lake.label: Great Bear Lake
great-bear-lake.description: The vast subarctic lake of northern Canada, staging ground for Jaspar Hobson's expedition toward the Arctic coast in The Fur Country.

great-eyrie-investigation.label: The Investigation of the Great Eyrie

great-eyrie.label: The Great Eyrie
great-eyrie.description: The inaccessible crater-crowned mountain in the Blue Ridge that conceals Robur's secret in Master of the World.

great-salt-lake.label: Great Salt Lake
great-salt-lake.description: The real Utah salt lake and Mormon country the Pacific Railroad skirts as Phileas Fogg crosses America.
great-salt-lake.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

great-slave-lake.label: Great Slave Lake
great-slave-lake.description: The great sub-Arctic Canadian lake from whose shores the Hudson's Bay expedition of The Fur Country sets out for the polar coast.

greenland.label: Greenland
greenland.description: Great Arctic island whose ice-bound coasts recur across Verne's polar voyages as the threshold of the far north.

guamini.label: Guamini
guamini.description: A river and salt-lake district of the Argentine Pampas, near the Sierra Ventana, sought for water by the expedition in In Search of the Castaways.

gueboroa-island.label: Gueboroa Island
gueboroa-island.description: The Papuan island in the Torres Strait on which the Nautilus runs aground, and where Ned Land forages ashore, in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
gueboroa-island.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

guiana.label: Guiana
guiana.description: The northern coast of South America, split into French and Dutch Guiana, a longed-for shore for Verne's castaways.

guinea.label: Guinea
guinea.description: West African coastal region and the Gulf that bears its name, a landmark of Vernean African exploration.

gulf-of-mexico.label: Gulf of Mexico
gulf-of-mexico.description: The warm gulf where the Gulf Stream is born, described at length during the Nautilus voyage in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
gulf-of-mexico.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

gulf-stream.label: Gulf Stream
gulf-stream.description: The real warm-water Atlantic current followed by the Nautilus in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas, a river within the ocean traced by Captain Nemo.
gulf-stream.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

gun-club-moon-proposal.label: Barbicane's Moon Proposal
gun-club-moon-proposal.description: President Barbicane proposes to the Gun Club that they fire a shot at the Moon.
gun-club-moon-proposal.book: From the Earth to the Moon

gun-club.label: Gun Club
gun-club.description: The Baltimore artillery club that undertakes to fire a projectile to the Moon.
gun-club.book: From the Earth to the Moon

gun-cotton.label: Gun-Cotton
gun-cotton.description: Pyroxyle, the nitro-cellulose explosive Verne uses as propellant from the lunar cannon to the castaways of The Mysterious Island.
gun-cotton.book: From the Earth to the Moon

halbrane-land.label: Halbrane Land
halbrane-land.description: The fictional Antarctic land named by the shipwrecked crew of An Antarctic Mystery for their lost schooner, the Halbrane.

ham-rock.label: Ham Rock
ham-rock.description: The isolated reef, named by the castaways of the Chancellor, on which the burning ship grounds before they take to the raft in The Survivors of the Chancellor.

hamburg.label: Hamburg
hamburg.description: The old German city where A Journey to the Center of the Earth begins, home of Professor Liedenbrock and the starting point of the descent toward Iceland.
hamburg.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

havre.label: Havre
havre.description: The great French port on the Channel, gateway of Atlantic passage and home of many a Verne mariner and passenger.

healthful-house.label: Healthful House
healthful-house.description: A private sanitarium on the banks of the Neuse where the inventor Thomas Roch is interned in Facing the Flag.

herbert.label: Herbert
herbert.description: The youngest of the five castaways of Lincoln Island, the boy naturalist of the colony.

hercules.label: Hercules
hercules.description: The giant black companion of the Pilgrim castaways, whose immense strength and devotion repeatedly save his friends.
hercules.book: Dick Sand, A Captain at Fifteen

himalaya.label: Himalaya
himalaya.description: The mightiest mountain range on earth, radiating from the Pamir and crowned by the highest summits on the face of the globe.
himalaya.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

holland.label: Holland
holland.description: One of the six polar powers bidding for the Arctic regions at the Baltimore auction in Topsy-Turvy.

hong-kong.label: Hong Kong
hong-kong.description: The British colonial port and pivotal waypoint on Phileas Fogg's route in Around the World in Eighty Days.
hong-kong.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

hudsons-bay-company.label: Hudson's Bay Company
hudsons-bay-company.description: The chartered British fur-trading company whose expedition to found a factory beyond the Arctic Circle drives the plot of The Fur Country.

hudsons-bay.label: Hudson's Bay
hudsons-bay.description: The great inland sea of northern Canada, gateway for the fur-trade expeditions of the north — distinct from the trading company of the same name.

ice-bank.label: Ice Bank
ice-bank.description: The great southern ice barrier that halts the Nautilus at the limit of the Antarctic seas in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
ice-bank.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

ice-island-drift.label: The Drifting Ice-Island
ice-island-drift.description: An Arctic earthquake severs the Cape Bathurst peninsula from the mainland, turning the fur-traders' settlement into a floating island adrift on the polar sea.

iceland.label: Iceland
iceland.description: The northern island nation and gateway to the underworld in Journey to the Center of the Earth.
iceland.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

ichim.label: Ichim
ichim.description: A Western Siberian post-town on the road from Ekaterenburg to Irkutsk, a waypoint for the couriers and correspondents in Michael Strogoff.

icy-cape.label: Icy Cape
icy-cape.description: The Alaskan promontory near which the drifting Victoria Island passes, home to the Esquimaux who aid the castaways in The Fur Country.

illinois.label: Illinois
illinois.description: Midwestern state of the United States; residence of Dirk Peters at Vandalia and a waypoint of several Vernean journeys.

in-search-of-the-castaways.label: In Search of the Castaways
in-search-of-the-castaways.description: A Jules Verne novel in which Lord Glenarvan sails the world along the 37th parallel to rescue the shipwrecked Captain Grant.

in-the-year-2889.label: In the Year 2889
aliases: La Journée d'un journaliste américain en 2889
The Day of an American Journalist in 2889
in-the-year-2889.description: A Jules Verne futurist tale following one day in the life of Fritz Napoleon Smith, director of the Earth Chronicle, amid the marvels of the twenty-ninth century.

india.label: India
india.description: The great reversed triangle of British India, crossed by rail in three days on Phileas Fogg's journey in Around the World in Eighty Days.
india.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

indian-ocean.label: Indian Ocean
indian-ocean.description: The great ocean the Duncan crosses on the 37th parallel in In Search of the Castaways, and a candidate ground for the Britannia wreck.

inquiry-committee.label: Inquiry Committee
inquiry-committee.description: The official commission under President John Prestice that interrogated J.T. Maston to uncover the secret of the North Pole cannon.

iowa.label: Iowa
iowa.description: The prairie state where the survivors of Lincoln Island settle, crossed by the transcontinental railroad in the Vernean novels.

iquitos.label: Iquitos
iquitos.description: The Upper Amazon farm-village where the Garral family lives and from which the great raft voyage sets out in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
iquitos.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

ireland.label: Ireland
ireland.description: The western island of the British Isles that the Nautilus and the Great Eastern pass on their Atlantic courses in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
ireland.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

irkutsk.label: Irkutsk
irkutsk.description: The far capital of Eastern Siberia and the goal of the courier's mission in Michael Strogoff.

irtish.label: Irtish
irtish.description: A great river of Western Siberia in Michael Strogoff, running past Omsk and marking the frontier of the Tartar advance.

island-of-ljakow.label: Island of Ljakow
island-of-ljakow.description: The New Siberian island for which the Alaska races in pursuit of Tudor Brown in The Waif of the Cynthia.

island-of-sein.label: Island of Sein
island-of-sein.description: The rocky island off the Brittany coast, ringed by deadly reefs, on which the Alaska is wrecked in The Waif of the Cynthia.

isthmus-of-panama.label: Isthmus of Panama
isthmus-of-panama.description: The narrow land bridge joining the Americas and the ocean shortcut between Atlantic and Pacific in Verne's tales.
isthmus-of-panama.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

italy.label: Italy
italy.description: The Mediterranean peninsula swept away by the comet Gallia, whose survival the survivors hope for in Off on a Comet.

jacamar-wood.label: Jacamar Wood
jacamar-wood.description: The forest on the left bank of the Mercy on Lincoln Island, named for the jacamar bird.

jangada.label: Jangada
jangada.description: The enormous timber raft, a floating village, that carries the Garral family down the Amazon in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
jangada.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

japan.label: Japan
japan.description: The Pacific empire beyond Hong Kong where Passepartout is stranded and rejoins Fogg, a stage of the circuit in Around the World in Eighty Days.
japan.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

JCE-ch01.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

JCE-ch02.label: A Journey to the Center of the Earth — CHAPTER II
JCE-ch02.description: A MYSTERY TO BE SOLVED AT ANY PRICE
JCE-ch02.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

JCE-ch03.label: A Journey to the Center of the Earth — CHAPTER III
JCE-ch03.description: THE RUNIC WRITING EXERCISES THE PROFESSOR
JCE-ch03.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

JCE-ch04.label: A Journey to the Center of the Earth — CHAPTER IV

journey-to-the-center-of-the-earth.label: A Journey to the Center of the Earth
aliases: Journey to the Center of the Earth
A Journey to the Centre of the Earth
journey-to-the-center-of-the-earth.description: A Jules Verne novel in which Professor Liedenbrock descends an Icelandic volcano toward the center of the earth.

kachgar.label: Kachgar
kachgar.description: The capital of Chinese Turkestan and the junction of the Russian and Chinese lines of the Grand Transasiatic railway in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
kachgar.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

kamtchatka-current.label: Kamtchatka Current
kamtchatka-current.description: The cold Bering ocean current running along the Asian coast, which seized the drifting ice-island and swept it toward the Arctic solitudes in The Fur Country.

kamtchatka.label: Kamtchatka
kamtchatka.description: The far-eastern Siberian peninsula whose cold ocean current threatens the drifting ice-island in The Fur Country.

kamtschatka.label: Kamtschatka
kamtschatka.description: The far-eastern Russian peninsula of volcanoes and the Behring Sea, a remote march of the Muscovite dominion in Verne's Asia.

kazeh.label: Kazeh
kazeh.description: An important establishment in the interior of Central Africa, a rendezvous for traders and caravans reached by Dr. Ferguson's balloon in Five Weeks in a Balloon.

kazounde.label: Kazounde
kazounde.description: The great inland slave-market town of Angola where the captives are driven and sold in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen.
kazounde.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

kentucky.label: Kentucky
kentucky.description: Southern US state, home of the Mammoth Cave, Verne's recurring benchmark for subterranean grandeur.

kerguelen.label: Kerguelen
kerguelen.description: The desolate southern island group where the narrator joins the Halbrane in An Antarctic Mystery.

ki-tsang-train-attack.label: Ki-Tsang's Attack on the Grand Transasiatic
aliases: The Attack on the Grand Transasiatic
ki-tsang-train-attack.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

kilimanjaro-cannon-shot.label: The Firing of the Kilimanjaro Cannon

kilimanjaro.label: Kilimanjaro
kilimanjaro.description: East African mountain into whose flank the Gun Club bores its colossal cannon in Topsy Turvy.

klock-klock.label: Klock-Klock
klock-klock.description: Native village in a valley of Tsalal Island where the deadly landslip of An Antarctic Mystery is investigated.

kolyvan.label: Kolyvan
kolyvan.description: A Western Siberian town on the telegraph line between Tomsk and Krasnoiarsk, and the place where Michael Strogoff is captured in Michael Strogoff.

krasnoiarsk.label: Krasnoiarsk
krasnoiarsk.description: Eastern Siberian town on the Yenisei where Michael Strogoff hopes to find fresh transport for Irkutsk.

labrador.label: Labrador
labrador.description: The bleak sub-Arctic coast of northeastern Canada, a waymark of the northern voyages and namesake of tundra plant and stone.

lake-baikal.label: Lake Baikal
lake-baikal.description: The great Siberian lake Michael Strogoff must skirt on his race to Irkutsk, stormy and freezing as winter closes in.

lake-barnett.label: Lake Barnett
lake-barnett.description: The lake on the drifting Victoria Island, named for Mrs Barnett, whose fate mirrors the doomed colony in The Fur Country.

lake-erie.label: Lake Erie
lake-erie.description: The real Great Lake from whose depths Robur's machine the Terror rises in The Master of the World, pursued across its waters by the narrator Strock.

lake-grant.label: Lake Grant
lake-grant.description: The tree-framed freshwater lake on the plateau of Lincoln Island, a central landmark of the colonists' domain in The Mysterious Island.

lake-kirdall.label: Lake Kirdall
lake-kirdall.description: A landlocked Kansas lake where Robur's submarine Terror is seen darting beneath the water in The Master of the World.

lake-michigan.label: Lake Michigan
lake-michigan.description: The Great Lake at whose shore the road-race track ends in The Master of the World, where the mysterious machine seems bound for destruction.

lake-ontario.label: Lake Ontario
lake-ontario.description: Lowest of the Great Lakes, into which the Niagara empties Lake Erie, recurring across Verne's American scenes.

lake-superior.label: Lake Superior
lake-superior.description: The largest of the Great Lakes, where the mysterious machine of The Master of the World is sighted before vanishing across the continent.

lake-tanganyika.label: Lake Tanganyika
lake-tanganyika.description: The great Central African lake that figures in Dick Sand as a hub of the slave trade and a landmark of Livingstone's explorations.
lake-tanganyika.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

lake-taupo.label: Lake Taupo
lake-taupo.description: The real New Zealand lake in whose mountain-ringed heights the Maori hold Glenarvan's party captive in In Search of the Castaways.

lake-tchad.label: Lake Tchad
lake-tchad.description: The great Central African lake that Dr. Ferguson's balloon expedition crosses in Five Weeks in a Balloon.

lake-tinn.label: Lake Tinn
lake-tinn.description: The long Norwegian lake of the Telemark whose shores frame the journeys and misfortunes of Ticket No. 9672.

lake-ukereoue.label: Lake Ukereoue
lake-ukereoue.description: Verne's name for the great equatorial African lake, conjectured reservoir of the Nile, sighted from Dr. Ferguson's balloon in Five Weeks in a Balloon.

lan-tcheou.label: Lan-Tcheou
lan-tcheou.description: An important city of western China on the Grand Transasiatic line, a halt in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent where the imperial treasure van is telegraphed to Pekin.
lan-tcheou.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

lancaster-strait.label: Lancaster Strait
lancaster-strait.description: The real strait off the west coast of Baffin's Sea, the historic gateway of the Northwest Passage explorers that the Forward enters on its polar course.

lena.label: Lena
lena.description: One of the great rivers of eastern Siberia, running north to an Arctic mouth that guided Nordenskiold's Vega and the Tartar march in Verne.

liedenbrock-cipher.label: Deciphering the Saknussemm Cipher
liedenbrock-cipher.description: Axel accidentally deciphers the runic parchment that reveals the way to the centre of the Earth.
liedenbrock-cipher.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

liedenbrock-sea.label: Liedenbrock Sea
liedenbrock-sea.description: The vast subterranean ocean discovered beneath Iceland in Journey to the Center of the Earth.
liedenbrock-sea.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

lima-indian-uprising.label: The Lima Indian Uprising
aliases: The Rising of the Limanian Indians

lima.label: Lima
lima.description: Peruvian capital in the valley of the Rimac, the setting of The Pearl of Lima.

lincoln-island-landing.label: The Lincoln Island Landing
lincoln-island-landing.description: The balloon castaways are hurled by a hurricane onto an unknown Pacific island.

lincoln-island.label: Lincoln Island
lincoln-island.description: The volcanic Pacific island where the balloon castaways settle and build their colony in The Mysterious Island.

lisbon.label: Lisbon
lisbon.description: The Portuguese capital and Atlantic port, seat of a colonial government implicated in Verne's tale of the slave trade.
lisbon.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

liverpool.label: Liverpool
liverpool.description: English port where Phileas Fogg lands from America, only six hours from the wager he seems to have lost.
liverpool.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

loch-katrine.label: Loch Katrine
loch-katrine.description: The real Scottish loch above the New Aberfoyle colliery in The Underground City, drained when its bed gives way into the mine below.

loch-malcolm.label: Loch Malcolm
loch-malcolm.description: The subterranean lake in the depths of New Aberfoyle, on whose banks the mining settlement of Coal Town grows up in The Underground City.

london.label: London
london.description: The English capital and home of Phileas Fogg, where his wager begins and must end in Around the World in Eighty Days.
london.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

longs-peak.label: Long's Peak
longs-peak.description: A summit of the Rocky Mountains where the Gun Club raised its gigantic reflecting telescope to track the lunar projectile.
longs-peak.book: From the Earth to the Moon

lunar-orbit-miss.label: Missing the Moon
lunar-orbit-miss.description: A stray asteroid deflects the projectile so that it circles the Moon instead of landing.

lunar-projectile.label: The Projectile
lunar-projectile.description: The hollow aluminium projectile-car in which three travellers are fired to the Moon by the Columbiad in From the Earth to the Moon and Round the Moon.

lyons.label: Lyons
lyons.description: The great French silk-city whose looms are a byword for quality and whose fabric forms the very skin of Verne's balloons.

madagascar.label: Madagascar
madagascar.description: The great Indian Ocean island that stands as a terminus of the slave-trade in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen, supplied from the Mozambique coast.
madagascar.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

madras.label: Madras
madras.description: The great southern Indian city and seat of a British governor, a cornerstone of the East India Company's dominion.
madras.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

madrid.label: Madrid
madrid.description: The Spanish capital, a discreet telegraphic relay in Verne's plots and a landmark swept by the wandering comet.

malta.label: Malta
malta.description: The fortified Mediterranean island of the knights and their great cannon, swallowed whole when a fragment of earth breaks away in Off on a Comet.

manaos.label: Manaos
manaos.description: The Brazilian city at the mouth of the Rio Negro, a chief landmark on the Garrals' raft voyage down the Amazon in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
manaos.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

mandible-cape.label: Mandible Cape
mandible-cape.description: The twin fictional capes, North and South Mandible, that frame the northern jaw of Lincoln Island's Union Bay in The Mysterious Island.

marseilles.label: Marseilles
marseilles.description: The great French Mediterranean port, a landfall for Verne's returning voyagers and a byword for the Provençal coast.
marseilles.book: A Journey to the Center of the Earth

maryland.label: Maryland
maryland.description: State of the United States, home to Baltimore and seat of the Gun Club in the Verne cannon novels.

massachusetts.label: Massachusetts
massachusetts.description: New England state; home of Cyrus Smith, and site of the Cambridge observatory in Verne's astronomical novels.

master-zacharius.label: Master Zacharius
aliases: Master Zacharius, or the Clockmaker Who Lost His Soul
The Clockmaker Who Lost His Soul
master-zacharius.description: A Jules Verne fantasy novella in which an aged Geneva clockmaker, believing his life bound to his watches, is destroyed by the pride of science and a diabolical bargain.

mediterranean.label: Mediterranean
mediterranean.description: The inland sea whose Algerian shore is torn away by the comet in Off on a Comet, and which the Nautilus also transits in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.

melbourne.label: Melbourne
melbourne.description: Australian port on Port Phillip that anchors the Duncan party's crossing of the Victorian continent.

melville-bay.label: Melville Bay
melville-bay.description: The real Arctic bay off Baffin's Sea toward which Hatteras first orders the Forward, the gateway to the far northern channels.

melville-island.label: Melville Island
melville-island.description: The Arctic island where Parry wintered in 1819, a recurring landmark of exploration history in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras.

mercy-river.label: The Mercy
mercy-river.description: The chief river of Lincoln Island, named by the castaways in gratitude to Providence.

merv.label: Merv
merv.description: The Transcaspian oasis city and Grand Transasiatic railway station, four times rebuilt, on the reporter's road to China.
merv.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

mexico.label: Mexico
mexico.description: The republic south of the United States whose parallel and gulf figure in the Gun Club schemes of From the Earth to the Moon.
mexico.book: From the Earth to the Moon

michael-strogoff.description: A Jules Verne novel in which a courier of the Czar races across Tartar-invaded Siberia to carry a vital letter from Moscow to Irkutsk.

michel-ardan.label: Michel Ardan
michel-ardan.description: The daring French adventurer who volunteers to ride the projectile to the Moon.
michel-ardan.book: From the Earth to the Moon

michel-strogoff.label: Michel Strogoff
michel-strogoff.description: The Czar's courier who carries a vital letter across rebel-held Siberia from Moscow to Irkutsk.

missionary-rescue.label: The Missionary's Rescue
aliases: The Rescue of the French Missionary
missionary-rescue.description: Dr. Ferguson's balloon Victoria descends over an African village to snatch a young French missionary from the stake, lifting him to safety just as his captors close in.

mississippi.label: Mississippi
mississippi.description: The great river of North America, the "king of rivers" crossed by Verne's travellers on their transcontinental journeys.
mississippi.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

mongolia.label: Mongolia
mongolia.description: The vast desert land of Central Asia crossed by the Grand Transasiatic, a march of bazaars, brigands and the old wall against China.
mongolia.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

mont-blanc.label: Mont Blanc
mont-blanc.description: The highest mountain in Europe, ascended from Chamonix in Verne's The Ascent of Mont Blanc.

montgomery-street.label: Montgomery Street
montgomery-street.description: The principal thoroughfare of San Francisco, lined with splendid stores and grand mansions, where the Kolderup family resides in Godfrey Morgan.

montreal.label: Montreal
montreal.description: The great Canadian city on the St. Lawrence, overflown by the Albatross and once the headquarters of the fur trade in Verne's tales.

morganton.label: Morganton
morganton.description: North Carolina town at the foot of the Great Eyrie, base of the narrator's investigation in The Master of the World.

morris-island.label: Morris Island
morris-island.description: The Federal-held island guarding the southern entrance to Charleston Harbor, whose batteries the Dolphin must run in The Blockade Runners.

moscow.label: Moscow
moscow.description: The old Russian capital where Michael Strogoff opens, the Kremlin city from which the Czar's courier sets out on his three-thousand-mile race to Irkutsk.

mount-franklin.label: Mount Franklin
mount-franklin.description: The volcano that dominates Lincoln Island in The Mysterious Island.

mount-mendif.label: Mount Mendif
mount-mendif.description: The twin-coned African peak near Yola that the Victoria passes on its balloon crossing in Five Weeks in a Balloon.

mounts-doerfel.label: Mounts Doerfel
mounts-doerfel.description: The lofty lunar mountain range near the Moon's south pole observed by the travellers in Round the Moon.

nantucket.label: Nantucket
nantucket.description: Island off Massachusetts, celebrated in An Antarctic Mystery as the birthplace of Arthur Gordon Pym.

nautilus.label: Nautilus
nautilus.description: Captain Nemo's electric submarine in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas.
nautilus.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

navy-island.label: Navy Island
navy-island.description: The island in the Niagara River above the falls, past which the Terror sweeps in The Master of the World.

neb.label: Neb
neb.description: One of the five castaways of Lincoln Island, the faithful servant of the engineer Cyrus Harding.

nebraska.label: Nebraska
nebraska.description: The prairie state of the American plains and eastern terminus of the Pacific Railroad in Around the World in Eighty Days.
nebraska.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

ned-land.label: Ned Land
ned-land.description: The Canadian master harpooner held captive aboard the Nautilus.
ned-land.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

negoro.label: Negoro
negoro.description: The treacherous Portuguese cook of the Pilgrim who sabotages the ship and reveals himself as a slave-trader.
negoro.book: Dick Sand, A Captain at Fifteen

nemo-death.label: The Death of Captain Nemo
nemo-death.description: Captain Nemo dies aboard the Nautilus in the grotto beneath Lincoln Island.

nemo-electricity.label: Nemo's Electricity
nemo-electricity.description: Captain Nemo's self-sufficient sea-derived electrical power that drives the Nautilus in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas.
nemo-electricity.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

nevada.label: Nevada
nevada.description: The silver state of the American Great Basin, crossed by the Pacific Railroad between the Sierra Nevada and the Great Salt Lake in Around the World in Eighty Days.
nevada.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

new-aberfoyle.label: New Aberfoyle
new-aberfoyle.description: The vast subterranean coalfield beneath old Aberfoyle, containing the underground settlement of Coal Town on Loch Malcolm.

new-america.label: New America
new-america.description: The unknown polar land discovered by the travellers, named New America by the American Altamont.

new-archangel.label: New Archangel
new-archangel.description: The capital of Russian America, rising on the island of Sitka as head-quarters of the Russian Fur Company.

new-berne.label: New-Berne
new-berne.description: The North Carolina port on the left bank of the Neuse near Pamlico Sound, off which the schooner Ebba lay while its master carried off the prisoners of Healthful House.

new-georgia.label: New Georgia
new-georgia.description: A sub-Antarctic island of the South Atlantic, a southern waypoint in An Antarctic Mystery.

new-orleans.label: New Orleans
new-orleans.description: The great Southern port at the mouth of the Mississippi, a hub of American commerce and travel in Verne's tales.
new-orleans.book: From the Earth to the Moon

new-york.label: New York
new-york.description: The great American Atlantic port and transatlantic gateway, hub of Verne's ocean crossings and inquiries.

new-zealand.label: New Zealand
new-zealand.description: Two-island British possession of the South Pacific, Maori land where Glenarvan's party is imperilled in In Search of the Castaways.

newfoundland.label: Newfoundland
newfoundland.description: The great North Atlantic island whose foggy fishing banks and telegraph landfall recur across Verne's Atlantic voyages.

niagara-falls.label: Niagara Falls
niagara-falls.description: The great cataract on the Niagara River where the pursuit of the Terror reaches its crisis in The Master of the World.

niagara-river.label: Niagara River
niagara-river.description: The real river draining Lake Erie into Lake Ontario, down whose rapids the Terror flees toward its celebrated falls in The Master of the World.

nijni-novgorod.label: Nijni-Novgorod
nijni-novgorod.description: Russian fair-city on the Volga where Michael Strogoff begins his eastward journey across Siberia.

nile.label: Nile
nile.description: The great African river whose mysterious sources drive the geographic quest of Five Weeks in a Balloon, sought for centuries and never reached from the ground.

nitroglycerine.label: Nitroglycerine
nitroglycerine.description: The powerful liquid explosive synthesized by Cyrus Harding on Lincoln Island in The Mysterious Island.

norfolk-island.label: Norfolk Island
norfolk-island.description: The South Pacific penal island from which Bob Harvey's convicts escape to menace the colonists in The Mysterious Island.

noroe.label: Noroe
noroe.description: Fishing village on the western coast of Norway near Bergen where the foundling Erik is raised in The Waif of the Cynthia.

north-carolina.label: North Carolina
north-carolina.description: The southern American state, capital Raleigh, whose Alleghany heights hold the Great Eyrie and whose coast borders Pamlico Sound in Master of the World and Facing the Flag.

north-polar-practical-association.label: North Polar Practical Association
north-polar-practical-association.description: The Baltimore company, a front for the Gun Club, that buys the North Pole at auction in order to exploit the coal buried beneath the polar ice.

north-pole-volcano.label: The Volcano at the Pole
north-pole-volcano.description: Hatteras reaches the North Pole and finds it crowned by an active volcano.

north-pole.label: The North Pole
north-pole.description: The volcanic island at the geographic North Pole, goal of the expedition in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras.

north-sea.label: North Sea
north-sea.description: Shallow arm of the Atlantic between Britain and the European mainland, recurring across several of Verne's voyages.
north-sea.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

north-west-company.label: North-West Company
north-west-company.description: The Montreal fur-trading company that rivalled the Hudson's Bay Company until it was absorbed in 1821.

north-west-passage.label: North-West Passage
north-west-passage.description: The famous Arctic sea route giving direct communication between the Atlantic and Pacific oceans, undiscovered until McClure won it and a lodestar for the polar voyages of Verne northern tales.

norway.label: Norway
norway.description: Scandinavian kingdom of fjords and mountains, principal setting of Ticket No. 9672 and homeland of several Verne seafarers.

obi.label: Obi
obi.description: Great West Siberian river Michael Strogoff must cross near Kolyvan on his road to Irkutsk.

off-on-a-comet.label: Off on a Comet
aliases: To the Sun? / Off on a Comet
off-on-a-comet.description: A Jules Verne novel in which a comet grazes the Earth and carries Captain Hector Servadac off into space on a fragment named Gallia.

ohio.label: Ohio
ohio.description: State of the United States on the southern shore of Lake Erie; the theatre of the pursuit of the Terror in The Master of the World.

omaha.label: Omaha
omaha.description: Nebraska terminus of the Union Pacific where Phileas Fogg boards the transcontinental railroad across America.
omaha.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

omsk.label: Omsk
omsk.description: The Western Siberian city and military centre that is Michael Strogoff's home town and an early goal of his journey.

oxyhydric-gas.label: oxyhydric gas
oxyhydric-gas.description: The illuminating gas Doctor Ox uses as cover for his physiological experiment on the town of Quiquendone.

pacific-ocean.label: Pacific Ocean
pacific-ocean.description: The vast ocean whose islands close the search of In Search of the Castaways, where the castaways are finally sought and found.

pacific-railroad.label: Pacific Railroad
pacific-railroad.description: The American transcontinental railway that carries Phileas Fogg across the continent in Around the World in Eighty Days.
pacific-railroad.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

paganel.label: Paganel
paganel.description: The absent-minded geographer who joins the search for Captain Grant after boarding the wrong ship.

pamir.label: Pamir
pamir.description: High plateau of Central Asia, the "Roof of the World," pierced by the Grand Transasiatic railway in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
pamir.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

pamlico-sound.label: Pamlico Sound
pamlico-sound.description: The real North Carolina sound where the schooner Ebba lies anchored in Facing the Flag, and across which the kidnappers spirit their captive away.

paris.label: Paris
paris.description: The French capital, first Continental stage of Fogg's circuit and a recurring metropolis in Verne's tales.
paris.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

passepartout.label: Passepartout
passepartout.description: The good-humoured French valet who accompanies Phileas Fogg around the world.
passepartout.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

patagonia.label: Patagonia
patagonia.description: Southern region of South America crossed on the 37th parallel in the first phase of the search for Captain Grant.

peak-of-teneriffe.label: Peak of Teneriffe
peak-of-teneriffe.description: The great volcanic peak of the Canary Islands, sighted from the Duncan early in the voyage of In Search of the Castaways.

pekin.label: Pekin
pekin.description: The Chinese capital that is the eastern terminus of the Grand Transasiatic railway journey in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
pekin.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

pencroft.label: Pencroft
pencroft.description: The sailor among the five castaways of Lincoln Island, the practical mainstay and shipbuilder of the colony.

peninsular-and-oriental-company.label: Peninsular and Oriental Company
peninsular-and-oriental-company.description: The British steamship company whose liners Mongolia and Rangoon carry Phileas Fogg across the eastern legs of his journey in Around the World in Eighty Days.

pennsylvania.label: Pennsylvania
pennsylvania.description: Mid-Atlantic state; home of the Weldon Institute in Philadelphia in Robur the Conqueror.

perm.label: Perm
perm.description: Russian Ural province and town, the last stage west of the mountains on Michael Strogoff's road into Siberia.

persia.label: Persia
persia.description: The storied land skirted by the Grand Transasiatic Railway in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent, and the source of the imperial treasure sent from the Shah of Persia to China.

perth.label: Perth
perth.description: The capital of Western Australia and seat of a convict penitentiary from which an escaped band menaces the castaways.

peru.label: Peru
peru.description: The Andean republic from whose forests the Upper Amazon issues and where Joam Dacosta took refuge for years in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
peru.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

philadelphia.label: Philadelphia
philadelphia.description: The Pennsylvania city home to the Weldon Institute of aeronautics, whose members clash with Robur in Robur the Conqueror.

phileas-fogg.label: Phileas Fogg
phileas-fogg.description: The precise English gentleman who wagers he can circle the globe in eighty days.
phileas-fogg.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

plaza-mayor.label: Plaza-Mayor
plaza-mayor.description: The great central square of Lima, the forum of the ancient city of kings, fronted by the viceregal palace and swept by the tumult of the Indian revolt.

pleasant-garden.label: Pleasant Garden
pleasant-garden.description: The North Carolina village below the Great Eyrie, reached from Morganton along the Catawba River, whose folk were menaced by the mountain in Master of the World.

polar-sea.description: The Arctic Ocean along the northern coast of America, on whose shifting ice Fort Hope is founded and set adrift in The Fur Country.

port-balloon.label: Port Balloon
port-balloon.description: A sheltered creek on the southern coast of Lincoln Island that the colonists adopt as their natural harbour in The Mysterious Island.

port-barnett.description: The little harbour at Cape Bathurst founded by the Hudson's Bay expedition in The Fur Country, named for Mrs Paulina Barnett and lost when the cape drifts out to sea.

port-egmont.description: The real Falklands harbour where the schooner Halbrane provisions and enlists the enigmatic Hunt before sailing for the Antarctic in An Antarctic Mystery.

port-grauben.description: The creek on the shore of the underground Liedenbrock Sea, named by Professor Liedenbrock as the launch point of the raft voyage in Journey to the Centre of the Earth.

portugal.label: Portugal
portugal.description: The old colonial kingdom whose empire — reaching Brazil, Angola, and the Azores — shadows Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon and other Verne voyages.

professor-lidenbrock.label: Professor Lidenbrock
professor-lidenbrock.description: The impetuous German professor who leads the descent to the centre of the earth.

prospect-heights.label: Prospect Heights
prospect-heights.description: The high granite plateau above the Chimneys on Lincoln Island, home to Granite House and the colonists' gardens.

prussia.label: Prussia
prussia.description: The North German kingdom and great power, a workshop of industry and a party to the wars and treaties of Verne's Europe.

quebec.label: Quebec
quebec.description: The old French-founded Canadian city on the St. Lawrence, hometown of the harpooner Ned Land.

quiquendone.label: Quiquendone
quiquendone.description: A tiny, phlegmatic Flemish town, absent from every map, where Doctor Ox conducts his gas experiment.

raleigh.label: Raleigh
raleigh.description: The capital of North Carolina, an important inland town about one hundred and fifty miles within the province, reached by Strock in his pursuit of the Great Eyrie mystery.
