red-creek.label: Red Creek
red-creek.description: The fictional watercourse on Lincoln Island in The Mysterious Island, named for the colour of its waters and feeding the island's great lake.

red-sea.label: Red Sea
red-sea.description: The real sea between Africa and Arabia crossed by the Nautilus in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas, entered through Bab el Mandeb.

reform-club.label: Reform Club
reform-club.description: The Pall Mall gentlemen's club where Phileas Fogg makes and settles his wager to circle the globe in Around the World in Eighty Days.

reptile-end.label: Reptile End
reptile-end.description: The south-western cape of Lincoln Island, marking one extremity of the settlers' explored shore.

rhine.label: Rhine
rhine.description: The great river of the German lands, a natural frontier and a river of legend in Verne, seen as an unrolled ribbon from the aeronaut's car.

richmond.label: Richmond
richmond.description: The Confederate capital of Virginia besieged by Grant, the prison-city from which the castaways of Lincoln Island escape by balloon.

riga.label: Riga
riga.description: The Livonian city on the Baltic, home of Nadia Fedor before the long road into Siberian exile toward Irkutsk.

rio-colorado.label: Rio Colorado
rio-colorado.description: A Patagonian river crossing the thirty-seventh parallel, sister to the Rio Negro, searched by the expedition in In Search of the Castaways.

rio-de-janeiro.label: Rio de Janeiro
rio-de-janeiro.description: The Brazilian capital and seat of justice on the Atlantic, whose distant authority governs the fate of the Amazon travellers.
rio-de-janeiro.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

rio-de-la-plata.label: Rio de la Plata
rio-de-la-plata.description: The wide South American estuary of the River Plate, giving its name to the provinces of La Plata, passed by the Nautilus off Uruguay.
rio-de-la-plata.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

rio-negro.label: Rio Negro
rio-negro.description: The greatest black-water tributary of the Amazon, joining the main river at Manaos in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
rio-negro.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

RM-ch08.description: AT SEVENTY-EIGHT THOUSAND ONE HUNDRED AND FOURTEEN LEAGUES.

RM-ch14.description: A NIGHT OF THREE HUNDRED AND FIFTY-FOUR HOURS AND A HALF.

robur-the-conqueror.label: Robur the Conqueror
robur-the-conqueror.description: A Jules Verne novel in which the inventor Robur challenges the balloonists of the Weldon Institute with his heavier-than-air flying machine.

robur.label: Robur
robur.description: The defiant aeronautical genius who proclaims mastery of the air across two novels.

rocky-mountains.label: Rocky Mountains
rocky-mountains.description: The great North American range crowned by the Long's Peak observatory, from which the Gun Club tracks the lunar projectile across Verne's moon novels.

round-the-moon.label: Round the Moon
round-the-moon.description: Jules Verne's sequel to From the Earth to the Moon, following three travellers sealed in a projectile as it circles the Moon and returns to Earth.

royal-geographical-society.label: Royal Geographical Society
royal-geographical-society.description: The London learned society that funds and honours the exploratory expeditions of Verne's adventurers, from Ferguson's balloon voyage to Hatteras's polar journey.

royal-institution.label: Royal Institution
royal-institution.description: The Edinburgh learned society of which engineer James Starr is a leading member.

royal-society.label: Royal Society
royal-society.description: The premier London learned society whose members and laureates figure across several of the Voyages.

ruhmkorff-apparatus.label: Ruhmkorff Apparatus
ruhmkorff-apparatus.description: The portable electric induction lamp carried by the explorers in A Journey to the Centre of the Earth for safe underground light.
ruhmkorff-apparatus.book: A Journey to the Centre of the Earth

russia.label: Russia
russia.description: The vast empire whose Siberian provinces the Czar's courier crosses to reach besieged Irkutsk in Michael Strogoff.

sacramento.label: Sacramento
sacramento.description: California capital and river, western reach of the Pacific Railroad in Around the World in Eighty Days.
sacramento.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

sahara.label: Sahara
sahara.description: The vast African desert the balloon must cross in Five Weeks in a Balloon, a nine-hundred-mile expanse of solitudes where caravans may go weeks without water.

saint-paul-de-loanda.label: Saint Paul de Loanda
saint-paul-de-loanda.description: The capital of the Portuguese colony on the west coast of Africa, a coastal goal of trans-continental journeys in Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen.
saint-paul-de-loanda.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

saint-pierre-miquelon.label: Saint-Pierre-Miquelon
saint-pierre-miquelon.description: The French island station from which the schooner Viking set sail, its departure fixing the last certain moment before an ill-fated voyage was lost.

salt-lake-city.label: Salt Lake City
salt-lake-city.description: The Mormon capital of Utah beside the Great Salt Lake, a striking waymark on the transcontinental railway.
salt-lake-city.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

samarkand.label: Samarkand
samarkand.description: Ancient Central Asian city on the Grand Transasiatic railway, a principal stop on the narrator's journey in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
samarkand.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

san-francisco.label: San Francisco
san-francisco.description: The Californian port where Phileas Fogg lands in America and begins his rail crossing of the continent in Around the World in Eighty Days.
san-francisco.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

sandwich-islands.label: Sandwich Islands
sandwich-islands.description: The far-southern isles, discovered by Cook, that the Halbrane takes as her point of departure toward the pole in An Antarctic Mystery.

sardinia.label: Sardinia
sardinia.description: The great Mediterranean island south of Corsica, its barren rocks swept away in the comet's passage.

sargasso-sea.label: Sargasso Sea
sargasso-sea.description: The weed-choked Atlantic region the Nautilus crosses in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas.
sargasso-sea.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

saville-row.label: Saville Row
saville-row.description: The London street in Burlington Gardens where the methodical Phileas Fogg kept his house at No. 7, the starting point of his wager journey around the world.
saville-row.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

scotland.label: Scotland

sea-of-rains.description: The lunar plain Mare Imbrium, ringed by the Apennines and Carpathians, over which the projectile passes in Round the Moon.

siberia.label: Siberia
siberia.description: The vast Asiatic Russian land across which Michael Strogoff carries the Tsar's message from Moscow to Irkutsk.

sicily.label: Sicily
sicily.description: The great Mediterranean island crowned by Etna, marking an ancient land-bridge beneath the sea, torn from the map in Off on a Comet.

sierra-tandil.label: Sierra Tandil
sierra-tandil.description: A mountain range of the Argentine pampas rising a thousand feet above the sea, a landmark on the trans-Patagonian search in In Search of the Castaways.

aliases: The Firedamp Attack on New Aberfoyle

singapore.label: Singapore

smiths-straits.label: Smith's Straits
smiths-straits.description: The high Arctic passage, route of the American explorer Kane, that Shandon is ordered to attempt in the opening voyage of The Adventures of Captain Hatteras.

snaefells-descent.label: The Snæfellsjökull Descent
snaefells-descent.description: The explorers plunge into the crater of Snæfellsjökull to begin their journey underground.
snaefells-descent.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

snaefellsjokull.label: Snæfellsjökull
snaefellsjokull.description: The Icelandic glacier-volcano whose crater is the gateway to the interior of the Earth in Journey to the Center of the Earth.

snowy-river.label: Snowy River
snowy-river.description: The flood-swollen Australian river that bars the searchers' final approach to Twofold Bay in In Search of the Castaways.

sou-tcheou.label: Sou-Tcheou
sou-tcheou.description: A walled Chinese city on the Grand Transasiatic line, marked in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent as the western end of the Great Wall of China.
sou-tcheou.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

soudan.label: Soudan
soudan.description: Historic region of central Africa crossed by the balloon Victoria and a recurring frontier of Vernean exploration.

south-america.label: South America
south-america.description: The southern continent of the New World, a recurring stage across Verne's voyages from Patagonia to the Amazon.
south-america.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

south-pole-attained.label: The South Pole Attained
south-pole-attained.description: Captain Nemo drives the Nautilus beneath the Antarctic ice to reach the geographic South Pole and plant his flag on 21 March 1868.
south-pole-attained.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Seas

south-pole.label: South Pole
south-pole.description: The southernmost point of the earth, reached and claimed by Captain Nemo in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
south-pole.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

spain.label: Spain
spain.description: The Iberian kingdom from whose coast the comet Gallia carries off a band of Spaniards in Off on a Comet.

spencer-island.label: Spencer Island
spencer-island.description: The fictional Pacific island where Godfrey Morgan is marooned; bought by the millionaire Kolderup, it is renamed Phina Island by its unwitting castaway.

spitzbergen.label: Spitzbergen
spitzbergen.description: The high-Arctic archipelago north of Norway, a gateway and waymark for Verne's polar expeditions.

st-louis-fur-company.label: St Louis Fur Company
st-louis-fur-company.description: The American fur-trading company that is the sole remaining rival of the Hudson's Bay Company in the far north.

stapi.label: Stapi
stapi.description: An Icelandic village at the foot of Snæfell, the last staging point before the descent into the volcano.
stapi.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

stockholm.label: Stockholm
stockholm.description: The Swedish capital and home of Dr. Schwaryencrona, where the foundling Erik is raised and educated in The Waif of the Cynthia.

stony-hill.label: Stony Hill
stony-hill.description: The barren Florida hill where the Gun Club casts and buries its giant Columbiad cannon in From the Earth to the Moon.
stony-hill.book: From the Earth to the Moon

strait-of-gibraltar.label: Strait of Gibraltar
strait-of-gibraltar.description: The narrow passage between the Mediterranean and the Atlantic through which the Nautilus races in Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea.
strait-of-gibraltar.book: Twenty Thousand Leagues Under the Sea

strait-of-magellan.label: Strait of Magellan
strait-of-magellan.description: The southern passage between the Atlantic and Pacific through which the Duncan sails on her search in In Search of the Castaways.

strogoff-blinding.label: The Blinding of Michael Strogoff
strogoff-blinding.description: Condemned as a spy by the Tartar Emir, the Tsar's courier is sentenced to be blinded by a white-hot blade passed before his eyes at Tomsk.

stromboli-eruption-return.label: The Stromboli Eruption
stromboli-eruption-return.description: The explorers are hurled back to the surface by a volcanic eruption at Stromboli.
stromboli-eruption-return.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

stromboli.label: Stromboli
stromboli.description: The Italian volcano through whose crater the explorers are expelled at the end of Journey to the Center of the Earth.
stromboli.book: Journey to the Center of the Earth

suez.label: Suez
suez.description: Egyptian canal town where detective Fix first waits to intercept Phileas Fogg on the road to India.
suez.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

sullivan-island.label: Sullivan Island
sullivan-island.description: The Confederate-held island on the north side of Charleston Harbor, whose channel the Dolphin uses to slip the blockade in The Blockade Runners.

survivors-of-the-chancellor.label: The Survivors of the Chancellor
survivors-of-the-chancellor.description: A Jules Verne novel told as the journal of a passenger aboard the burning ship Chancellor and the raft of its starving survivors.

sweden.label: Sweden
sweden.description: The Scandinavian homeland tied to Erik Hersebom and to the Nordenskiold arctic expedition in The Waif of the Cynthia.

switzerland.label: Switzerland
switzerland.description: The Alpine land of Master Zacharius, famed across Verne's works for its clockmakers, mountains, and glaciers.

sydney.label: Sydney
sydney.description: The great port of New South Wales and former capital of the eastern Australian colony, a hub of the castaways' wanderings.

tabatinga.label: Tabatinga
tabatinga.description: The first Brazilian town on the Amazon at the Peruvian frontier, a frontier post reached by the jangada in Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon.
tabatinga.book: Eight Hundred Leagues on the Amazon

tabor-island-castaway.label: The Tabor Island Castaway
tabor-island-castaway.description: The colonists find a man on Tabor Island reduced by solitude to a savage state.

tabor-island.label: Tabor Island
tabor-island.description: A small desert island in the South Pacific where the outlaw Ayrton was marooned, discovered by the Lincoln Island colonists.

tachkend.label: Tachkend
tachkend.description: A great city of Russian Turkestan on the Grand Transasiatic railway, a two-and-a-half-hour halt on the reporter's journey in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
tachkend.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

tadorn-marsh.label: Tadorn Marsh
tadorn-marsh.description: A vast marsh in the south-eastern part of Lincoln Island, a haunt of waterfowl and game.

tai-youan.label: Tai-Youan
tai-youan.description: A station city of northern China on the Grand Transasiatic line, the depot from which a relief engine is summoned in The Adventures of a Special Correspondent.
tai-youan.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

tampa-town.label: Tampa Town
tampa-town.description: The South Florida town chosen by the Gun Club as the launch site for its lunar projectile in From the Earth to the Moon.
tampa-town.book: From the Earth to the Moon

tasmania.label: Tasmania
tasmania.description: The English island south of Australia, a southern waymark of the whaling grounds and a byword for the convict colonies.

teheran.label: Teheran
teheran.description: The Persian capital beneath the snows of the Elburz, a byword for the fabrics of the East and a terminus of the great Asian railways.
teheran.book: The Adventures of a Special Correspondent

texas.label: Texas
texas.description: The vast southern Union state that vied with Florida to host the Gun Club Columbiad in From the Earth to the Moon.
texas.book: From the Earth to the Moon

thames.label: Thames
thames.description: The great river of London, crowded with barges and steamboats, down which the Resolute sailed toward the sea in Verne.

the-adventures-of-captain-hatteras.label: The Adventures of Captain Hatteras
the-adventures-of-captain-hatteras.description: A Jules Verne novel following the obsessive Captain Hatteras on his quest to reach the North Pole.

the-ascent-of-mont-blanc.label: The Ascent of Mont Blanc
the-ascent-of-mont-blanc.description: A Jules Verne first-person narrative recounting a perilous 1871 ascent of Mont Blanc from Chamonix, across glaciers, ice-walls, and avalanches to the summit and back.

the-blockade-runners.label: The Blockade Runners
the-blockade-runners.description: A Jules Verne novella in which a Glasgow captain runs the Union blockade of Charleston for cotton profit and stays to rescue an imprisoned abolitionist and his daughter.

the-chimneys.label: The Chimneys
the-chimneys.description: The castaways' first shelter on Lincoln Island: a natural pile of granite rocks at the mouth of the Mercy.

the-forward.label: The Forward
the-forward.description: The ice-armoured brig built for Captain Hatteras's secret voyage to the North Pole in The Adventures of Captain Hatteras.

the-fur-country.label: The Fur Country
aliases: The Fur Country: Seventy Degrees North Latitude
the-fur-country.description: A Jules Verne novel in which a Hudson Bay fur outpost at Cape Bathurst is set adrift when the ground beneath it proves to be floating ice.

the-master-of-the-world.label: The Master of the World
the-master-of-the-world.description: A Jules Verne novel in which a federal inspector pursues Robur and his all-terrain machine the Terror, whose inventor proclaims himself master of the world.

the-moon.label: The Moon
the-moon.description: Earth's satellite and the destination of the Gun Club's projectile across From the Earth to the Moon and Round the Moon.

the-mysterious-island.label: The Mysterious Island
the-mysterious-island.description: A Jules Verne novel in which five castaways build a colony on an uncharted Pacific island.

the-pearl-of-lima.label: The Pearl of Lima
aliases: The Pearl of Lima: A Story of True Love

the-sword.description: The small British submersible torpedo-boat sent into Back Cup lagoon to rescue Thomas Roch in Facing the Flag.

the-terror.label: The Terror
the-terror.description: Robur's four-mode machine in Master of the World, able to travel as automobile, boat, submarine, and aircraft.

the-waif-of-the-cynthia.label: The Waif of the Cynthia

weldon-institute.label: Weldon Institute
weldon-institute.description: The Philadelphia ballooning club whose lighter-than-air convictions are challenged by Robur and his flying machine the Albatross.

wellington-channel.label: Wellington Channel
wellington-channel.description: The real Arctic channel between North Devon and Cornwallis Island, one of the routes by which Hatteras seeks the open polar sea.

whale-hunt-disaster.label: The Whale Hunt Disaster
whale-hunt-disaster.description: Captain Hull and the whole crew of the Pilgrim perish in a single boat while hunting a jubarte whale, leaving the ship in the hands of the fifteen-year-old Dick Sand.
whale-hunt-disaster.book: Dick Sand: A Captain at Fifteen

wimerra.label: Wimerra
wimerra.description: A river and district of the Province of Victoria, crossed by the expedition in In Search of the Castaways.

wisconsin.label: Wisconsin
wisconsin.description: The northern state whose roads host the great automobile race of The Master of the World.

yakutsk.label: Yakutsk
yakutsk.description: Easternmost of the great governments of Asiatic Russia, whose relief column marches on Irkutsk in Michael Strogoff.

YEAR.label: In the Year 2889
YEAR.description: Little though they seem to think of it, the people of this twenty-ninth century live continually in fairyland.

yenisei.label: Yenisei
yenisei.description: Broad Eastern Siberian river below Krasnoiarsk that forms a major barrier on the road to Irkutsk.

yokohama.label: Yokohama
yokohama.description: The principal Japanese port where Phileas Fogg lands and re-embarks on his circuit of the globe in Around the World in Eighty Days.
yokohama.book: Around the World in Eighty Days

zabediero.label: Zabediero
zabediero.description: Siberian village on the Tom near Tomsk where Ivan Ogareff makes his forward camp and Michael Strogoff is taken prisoner.

zanzibar.label: Zanzibar
zanzibar.description: The East African island and port from which Dr. Ferguson launches his balloon voyage across the continent in Five Weeks in a Balloon.
